mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals contemporaneous expansion in real corporate output, which directly lifts expected earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive following month-over-month increases in INDPRO, or the rank correlation between INDPRO change and forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, restrictive monetary policy compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in consumer prices and the federal funds rate signals stagflationary pressure that erodes real earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not negative when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS increase jointly, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (t-stat < 1.5) in a bivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the unemployment rate is below 5.5%, an unexpected increase in nonfarm payrolls signals labor market overheating that raises the probability of near-term monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of PAYEMS increase observations when UNRATE is below 5.5%, or the mean forward return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales reflects weakening private consumption, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months following a decline in RSAFS, or the rank correlation between RSAFS change and forward returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms broad-based aggregate demand expansion, which supports earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly changes in GDPC1 do not coincide with positive average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns, or the rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous month-over-month increase in housing starts and payroll employment confirms a durable goods-and-labor demand loop that boosts cyclical earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in HOUST and PAYEMS do not coincide with positive average 5-day S&P 500 forward returns, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production combined with stable or falling unemployment signals a positive supply-demand gap for labor and output, lifting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of INDPRO growth and stable or falling UNRATE does not produce positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction term coefficient is not positive and significant (t-stat < 1.5) in a 3-year rolling panel.
